steps.text: Amend appendix A–3 to part 60 by revising figure 4–3 under the heading '18.0 Tables, Diagrams, Flowcharts, and Validation Data' in method 4.
Amend appendix A–4 to part 60 by revising section 10.1.3 in method 7.
Amend appendix A–7 to part 60 by revising equation 19–5 in section 12.2.3.2 in method 19.
In method 25: Adding sections 12.9 and 12.9.1 through 12.9.16.
Revise figure 25–6 under method 25.
Purchase an opacity monitor that meets the specifications of ASTM D6216–20.
Obtain a certificate of conformance from the opacity monitor manufacturer.
Purchase an opacity monitor that complies with ASTM D6216–20 and obtain a certificate of conformance from the opacity monitor manufacturer.

Demonstrate, to the satisfaction of the Administrator or delegated agent, that the average opacity measured at the alternative location or path is equivalent to the opacity as measured at a location meeting the criteria of sections 8.1(2)(i) and (ii) of this method.
Simultaneously measure the opacities at the two locations or paths for a minimum period of time (e.g., 180-minutes) covering the range of normal operating conditions and compare the results.
Conduct a three-point calibration error test using three calibration attenuators that produce outlet pathlength corrected, single-pass opacity values shown in ASTM D6216–20, section 7.5.
Confirm the external audit device produces the proper zero value on the COMS data recorder.
Insert each calibration attenuator (low, mid, and high-level) into the external audit device.
Use method 29, method 30A, or method 30B in appendix A–8 to this part or ASTM Method D6784–16 as the RM for Hg concentration.
Conduct the RM test runs with paired or duplicate sampling systems and use the average of the vapor phase Hg concentrations measured by the two trains.
Conduct a minimum of nine RM test runs.
Use a minimum sample time of 2 hours for method 29 and ASTM D6784–16 and 30 minutes for methods 30A and 30B.
Correlate the CEMS and the RM test data as to the time and duration.

constraints.text: For method 29 and ASTM Method D6784–16, the paired RM trains must meet the relative deviation criteria.
If more than nine sets of RM test runs are performed, all data must be reported including the excluded test run data.

steps.text: Conduct an Absolute Calibration Audit (ACA) quarterly.
Conduct a Relative Accuracy Test Audit (RATA) yearly if applicable.
Conduct the RATA as described in the RA test procedure.
Analyze the appropriate performance audit samples received from the EPA.
Conduct an ACA as described in the calibration error test procedure.
Cease hazardous waste burning immediately if the RA from the RATA or the CE from the ACA exceeds the criteria.
Select the sampling port location and the number/location of traverse points at the inlet and outlet of the control device.
Measure O2 at the inlet and outlet of the control device.
Measure moisture content at the inlet and outlet of the control device as needed to determine formaldehyde or THC and O2 concentrations on a dry basis.

preconditions.text: Measurements to determine O2 concentration must be made at the same time as the measurements for formaldehyde or THC concentration.

steps.text: Select the sampling port location and the number/location of traverse points at the exhaust of the stationary RICE.

preconditions.text: If demonstrating compliance with the formaldehyde percent reduction requirement, measure formaldehyde at the inlet and the outlet of the control device.
If demonstrating compliance with the THC percent reduction requirement, measure THC at the inlet and the outlet of the control device.
